file_name: Li et al. - 2022 - Convolutional neural network with multiscale fusion and attention mechanism for skin diseases assist.json

did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper uses a Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) named MSFA-Net (Multiscale Feature Fusion and Attention Network) for melanoma segmentation in dermoscopy images. The method involves a multiscale feature fusion structure, asymmetric skip connection, and attention mechanism, trained on the ISIC2018 dataset.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The document is explicitly labeled as a 'Research Article' and presents original experimental work. It proposes a novel multiscale feature fusion network (MSFA-Net) for melanoma segmentation, details the methodology (encoder-decoder structure, attention mechanism), describes the experimental setup using the ISIC2018 dataset, and reports quantitative results comparing the proposed method against baseline models like U-Net and Deeplabv3+.

title: Convolutional Neural Network with Multiscale Fusion and Attention Mechanism for Skin Diseases Assisted Diagnosis
brief_summary: This study proposes MSFA-Net, a convolutional neural network with multiscale feature fusion and an attention mechanism for melanoma segmentation in dermoscopy images. The model utilizes a multiscale feature fusion structure, an asymmetric skip connection, and a calibration decoder to address challenges such as irregular lesion shapes and weak edge features. The authors trained and evaluated the network using the ISIC2018 dataset, which was divided into 1,814 training, 260 validation, and 520 test images. Performance was assessed using Dice similarity coefficient and Intersection over Union metrics, comparing the proposed method against baseline models like U-Net and Deeplabv3+. The results demonstrated that MSFA-Net achieved superior segmentation accuracy with significantly fewer parameters compared to existing state-of-the-art methods.

lesion_types_studied.details: The study focuses on melanoma segmentation using the ISIC2018 dataset, which contains 2,594 lesions. The task is binary segmentation (lesion vs. non-lesion/background) rather than multi-class classification of different skin diseases.

all_task_types.target: Pixel-level binary mask distinguishing melanoma lesion area from non-lesion/background
all_task_types.details: The paper explicitly states the task is 'melanoma segmentation' using the ISIC2018 dataset. The model MSFA-Net outputs a segmentation mask (Ypred) of size 224x224x1, which is compared pixel-by-pixel with the ground truth labels (Ylabel). Performance is evaluated using Intersection over Union (IoU) and Dice similarity coefficient, which are standard metrics for segmentation tasks.

datasets_used.name: ISIC2018
datasets_used.description: The International Skin Imaging Collaboration 2018 melanoma dataset, containing dermoscopy images of skin lesions.

use.training: Used for training the MSFA-Net model with data augmentation (random rotation, center clipping, color adjustment).
use.validation: Used to evaluate the optimal model and detect the test set during cross-validation.
use.testing: Used to test the final performance of MSFA-Net and compare it against baseline models (U-Net, Deeplabv3+).

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The paper did not report formal hypothesis testing, p-values, or significance tests. Performance was evaluated using deterministic metrics (Dice similarity coefficient and Intersection over Union) and qualitative visual comparisons against baseline models.

ablation_study_on_dilated_rates_and_scSE.description: Comparison of different dilated rate combinations (r1, r2, r3, r4, r5) and scSE placement (I, II, or both) on the test set.
ablation_study_on_dilated_rates_and_scSE.notes: The combination of rates (1, 3, 6, 12, 18) with scSE in both locations (I and II) yielded the highest Dice score (0.9248). The combination of rates (1, 3, 6, 12, 18) with scSE only in location II yielded the highest IoU (0.9128).

comparison_with_baselines.description: Comparison of the proposed MSFA-Net variants against U-Net and Deeplabv3+ on the test set.
comparison_with_baselines.notes: Note: There is a discrepancy in the provided text between Table 1 and Table 2 for MSFA-Net (I and II) Dice score (0.9248 in Table 1 vs 0.8248 in Table 2). Table 2 lists MSFA-Net (I and II) Dice as 0.8248, while Table 1 lists it as 0.9248. The IoU for MSFA-Net (I and II) is 0.8852 in both tables. MSFA-Net (II) achieved the highest Dice (0.9271) and IoU (0.9128) among all reported models in Table 2. MSFA-Net variants have significantly fewer parameters (0.16-0.57M) compared to U-Net (9.5M) and Deeplabv3+ (54.7M).

architecture_used.main_model: MSFA-Net (Multiscale Feature Fusion and Attention Network)

architecture_details.attention: scSE (spatial and channel squeeze-and-excitation) placed in locations I and/or II

training_details.loss_function: Weighted cross-entropy loss (combining loss for Ypred, Y', and Y'')
training_details.learning_rate_strategy: Two-stage learning rate optimization strategy (LRAA) based on Adam
training_details.hardware: Intel i7 processor (3.6 GHz), 32 GB RAM, NVIDIA Geforce RTX 3090 GPU (24G)

preprocessing_techniques: Normalization with mean and standard deviation

data_augmentation_techniques.details: Applied to expand the amount of data during preprocessing.
Applied to expand the amount of data during preprocessing.
Adjustment of contrast, hue, brightness, and saturation to account for color differences between skin lesion areas and surrounding normal skin.
Normalization with mean and standard deviation.

conclusions: The proposed MSFA-Net effectively addresses the challenges of melanoma segmentation, such as irregular lesion shapes, weak edge features, and small target sizes, by utilizing a multiscale feature fusion structure and an attention mechanism.

MSFA-Net achieves superior segmentation performance compared to baseline models like U-Net and state-of-the-art methods like Deeplabv3+, as evidenced by higher Dice similarity coefficients and Intersection over Union (IoU) scores on the ISIC2018 dataset.

The model significantly reduces the number of parameters (e.g., 0.16M to 0.57M for MSFA-Net variants) compared to U-Net (9.5M) and Deeplabv3+ (54.7M), making it a lightweight and efficient solution suitable for medical platform deployment.

The integration of the asymmetric skip connection structure (ASCS) and the calibration decoder helps bridge the semantic gap between encoder and decoder features, improving the accuracy of pixel-level predictions for lesions with low contrast and irregular boundaries.
The use of a weighted cross-entropy loss function and a two-stage learning rate optimization strategy (LRAA) contributes to more effective training and convergence of the network.
important_remarks: The study is limited to binary segmentation of melanoma using only the ISIC2018 dataset, lacking external validation on diverse datasets or other skin disease types.

There is a significant data discrepancy in the reported results: Table 1 lists the Dice score for MSFA-Net (I and II) as 0.9248, while Table 2 lists it as 0.8248, creating ambiguity regarding the model's true performance.
The paper does not report statistical significance tests (e.g., p-values) for the performance comparisons against baseline models like U-Net and Deeplabv3+.

The model was trained and tested on a single dataset split (1,814 training, 260 validation, 520 test) without mention of cross-validation or independent external test sets, raising concerns about generalizability.

The hardware specifications (Intel i7, 32GB RAM, RTX 3090) and framework (PyTorch) are provided, but computational efficiency metrics (FLOPs) are reported alongside parameter counts, which may not fully reflect real-world deployment latency on medical platforms.

The authors acknowledge that targets with small shapes, prominent shapes, and irregular edges pose challenges, and while MSFA-Net addresses these, the qualitative analysis is limited to visual comparisons in Figure 6 without quantitative error analysis for these specific subgroups.
